info.title: API Portal - API Proxy (CF)
info.description: The API allows you to decouple the application-facing APIs from your backend changes, shielding those APIs from backend code changes. This provides flexibility to application developers to continue calling the same API.

x-sap-shortText: Decouples an API from any backend changes

isPublished.description: Indicates whether the API Proxy is published or not.

isVersioned.description: Indicates whether the version is enabled for API Proxy or not.

name.description: Technical name of the API Proxy.

provider_name.description: Technical name of the API Proxy Provider.

service_code.description: APIProxy of the type REST, ODATA or SOAP.

state.description: It verifies whether the status of the API Proxy on the runtime is DEPLOYED or UNDEPLOYED.

status_code.description: Checks whether the API Proxy status is EXTERNAL or REGISTERED.

releaseStatus.description: Current state of the API Proxy.

version.description: Current version of the API Proxy.

title.description: Title of the API Proxy displayed on the Product.

sslInfo.description: It's the API Provider SSL configuration.

provider_id.description: It's the provider id associated with the API Proxy.

targetAPIProxyName.description: Name of the target API Proxy.

get.summary: API proxies on the API portal
get.description: Returns all the API proxies on the API portal. An API proxy anonymize any HTTP endpoints like REST, OData or SOAP and enhance it with policies, and routes.

parameters.name: $select
parameters.description: Select properties to be returned, see [OData Select](http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.0/errata02/os/complete/part1-protocol/odata-v4.0-errata02-os-part1-protocol-complete.html#_Toc406398297)

post.summary: API proxies on the API portal
post.description: Adds a new entity to the API proxies.

get.summary: API Proxy name used to identify the entity
get.description: Returns the entity with the specified name from the API proxies.

parameters.name: revisionID
parameters.description: Revision number of the API proxy

parameters.name: $select
parameters.description: Select properties to be returned, see [OData Select](http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/odata/v4.0/errata02/os/complete/part1-protocol/odata-v4.0-errata02-os-part1-protocol-complete.html#_Toc406398297)

patch.description: Allows multiple actions on an API proxy revision, based on the mode `query parameter`. Supported values for mode include saveasrevision, revertrevision, deploy, and undeploy.

parameters.name: payload
parameters.description:
Patch request 
 ***Note**: *When copying the code, remove the comments and keep only the payload relevant to the selected mode*

400.description: This API cannot be undeployed as it is the only API Proxy associated with the product.

404.description: Requested entity could not be found.

405.description: UPDATE operation not supported on APIProxy entity.

500.description: API Proxy Revision creation or revert is not allowed

delete.description:
Deletes the specified API proxy and its revision from the API portal. The behavior of the revision deletion depends on the selected `mode`.

-If the mode is set to `discarddraft`, the revision is deleted only if it is in a draft state.

-If the mode is `discardrevision`, the specified revision is deleted regardless of its state.

**Note**: That this operation is irreversible.

404.description: The specified API proxy does not exist or has been deleted. Please provide a valid API proxy and try again.

500.description: API proxy revision or draft deletion is not allowed.

post.description:
Group multiple requests into a single request payload. For more information, see [Batch Requests](https://help.sap.com/doc/5890d27be418427993fafa6722cdc03b/Cloud/en-US/OdataV2.pdf#page=152).

 ***Note:** *The "Try out" action is not supported for this request.*

parameters.name: payload
parameters.description:
Batch request 
***Note**: *While copying the code, please remove the comments from the examples.*
